title: Polymath surface routing declaration
description: Validates a per-surface deterministic routing declaration. Each routable surface (skill, workflow, or — from Phase 3 — tool) declares its own signals in a sidecar `routing.yaml` next to the surface (skills/tools) or under plugins/polymath-flows/routing/<name>.yaml (workflows). tools/build-surface-index.py is the SINGLE PRODUCER that compiles every declaration into plugins/polymath-core/data/route-signals.json (formerly hand-maintained) so the polymath-core route-hint hook can narrow a prompt to the smallest matching surface. Hard signals (url/regex/paths/diff) are what actually fire a hint; intents alone never fire. `kind`, `id`, and the proposed `surface` string are DERIVED from the file location, not declared here, so a declaration cannot drift from the surface it routes to.

schemaVersion.description: Optional declaration-schema version. The only accepted value is 1; bumping it is a deliberate, gated act.

tier.description: Routing-coverage tier. 'hard' = this surface commits to deterministic routability (a route-triggering fixture exists and the coverage ratchet counts it); 'soft' (default) = best-effort signals, no fixture obligation. Runtime scoring is identical — the tier feeds the SURFACE-1 declare-or-exempt ratchet, not the scorer.

not_intents.description: Veto phrasings: when any of these appears (case-insensitive) in the prompt, this surface is removed from scoring entirely — even if its positive signals also fired. Use to separate near-collision siblings (e.g. a deploy surface vetoing 'rollback').

repo_state.description: Repo-evidence probes that contextually boost this surface (+1, soft — never satisfies the hard-signal firing requirement). Each entry is a path probe evaluated against the repo root at SessionStart by write-repo-evidence.py (exact path, 'dir/' suffix for directories, or a glob like '*.tf'), cached in repo-evidence.json. The union of all declared probes is capped at 64 across the catalog.

events.description: Declarative event-time triggers (PostToolUse), compiled into route-signals.json `events` and evaluated by event-trigger.py — the data-driven replacement for that hook's hardcoded table. A rule fires only when BOTH regexes match: `command` against the tool command and `output` against the tool output (high precision: a passing run or a command merely naming a runner stays silent).

command.description: Regex the tool COMMAND must match (case-insensitive).

output.description: Regex the tool OUTPUT must match (case-insensitive).

why.description: Short human reason shown in the nudge ('Detected <why>').

note.description: One-line hint appended to the proposal.

url.description: Regex patterns matched (case-insensitive) against URLs found in the prompt. Hard signal (score 3).

regex.description: Regex patterns matched against the raw prompt text (e.g. CVE / GHSA / ticket keys). Hard signal (score 3).

paths.description: Case-insensitive substrings whose presence in the prompt hints this surface (e.g. 'package.json', 'docs/adrs/'). Hard signal worth 2; since the proposal threshold is 3, a path fires only together with a matching intent (it satisfies the hard-signal requirement but is not sufficient alone). Paths may legitimately overlap across surfaces; scoring disambiguates.

diff.description: When true, the presence of an inline unified diff in the prompt hints this surface. Hard signal worth 2; like paths, it fires only together with a matching intent (threshold is 3).

intents.description: Naive natural-language phrasings that raise this surface's score (soft signal, score 1). Never fire on their own — a hard signal must also be present. Must be globally unique across all surfaces (the disambiguation floor, enforced by build-surface-index.py --strict).

alt.description: A single named alternative surface to offer alongside the primary proposal (e.g. the lighter skill behind a heavier workflow). Form '<plugin>:<surface>'.

capabilities.description: Capability ids this surface needs at run time (Phase 2+). Resolved against .polymath/capabilities.yaml. Vocabulary is the closed set from registry/schemas/capabilities.schema.json.

trust.description: Graduated autonomy — consumed by polymath-pipeline: when a project declares routing.mode classify|enforce, its directive honors 'auto-headless' for READ-ONLY surfaces (run without propose-confirm, minimum intervention); in hint mode and for everything else, every surface still proposes-first (see run-workflow SKILL.md). 'propose' (default) = detect and propose, never auto-run. 'auto-headless' = declares eligibility to run without confirmation in a non-interactive session (CI / cron / -p). Unconditional 'auto' (auto-run even interactively) is intentionally NOT in this enum — it needs a per-surface write-scope analysis that does not exist yet, so the schema rejects it directly (this replaced the bespoke TRUST-1 gate).

chainsTo.description: Surface names this surface naturally hands off to on completion (Phase 4 arc chaining), e.g. respondToIncident -> incidentRetroToActions.
